Transcrição Completa: The Ultimate Guide To Building Peak Trust
[00:00] What's up everybody? Welcome in. Hope you guys are doing good. Uh weekly group call. We're going to be going through an absolute banger today. We got what we call an ultimate guide video which is going to be quite thorough. If you have questions, feel free to ask them as we go into the chat. Um I want to stay in my flow here because I got a lot of shit I got to cover with you guys. So without further ado, let's jump in.
[00:24] Okay. So, the ultimate guide to peak trust levels. This question was prompted by a question from Jamie where Jaime's question was essentially something along the lines of like, hey, what do you do for your main website?
[00:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Ultimate Guide to Peak Trust Levels"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Título principal em destaque, layout de apresentação profissional
[00:42] Uh, I'm thinking to myself, how the fuck are you even asking this question, Jamie? And it let me know how much more training I must need to make in order to make you guys aware of the ultimate perspective to think through when it comes to everything that you do that supports your sales process.
[01:08] So just recently, let me explain my my life to you currently. Okay? So I'm 31 years old currently. All right? And I used to be in high school an athlete. I played football, basketball, and baseball. I would run every fucking day of my life for so long.
[01:32] Then I got into money soon as I was 19 years old, you know, started uh, you know, skip skipped college, started doing straight to the money. Okay. And at one point I was a vegan. I was deathly thin, terrible blood work, super low test, 180 lbs at 6'5, terribly unhealthy.

NÚMEROS: 31 anos, 19 anos, 180 lbs, 6'5"
VISUAL: Timeline visual da jornada pessoal, transformação física, marcos de vida
[02:15] I get hit with this ad on Thursday night from a guy named John. John's ad hits me square in the fucking chest, dude. John speaks directly to exactly the word track I use in my head. This man mastered messaging.
[02:38] Look at this. Look at this in Telegram later and like watch it yourself so you can see what I'm talking about. Okay, but long story short, back to my point. So, I get hit with this ad from this John guy and he says out loud everything I think in my head.
[03:02] He's like, "Look, dude, you're rich now. You got money, but you want to be top 1% of everything." You know, like why would you want money? Why would you want to be the guy with money that's not also in like peak physical condition?

VISUAL: Conceito de elite, excelência em todas áreas, riqueza + saúde física
[03:45] And the little things that he says, like he's he peppers in a whole bunch of little things like, "Look, dude, you're rich. Like you don't need to eat fucking packed broccoli and rice and chicken in a little fucking uh box, you know? Like that's not what you need for food, you know?
[04:08] He's like, you're a rich guy now. You need like a fleet of fucking people. It reminded me of Carlton's pitch who's here in the inner circle. Okay. Carlton helps people lower their tax burden.
[04:28] You guys undermine messaging so much. When you can at the very least just consistently speak directly out loud about the problems and the word track that's going through your consumer's mind, there's a much higher probability of response.

[50:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Negative Review Response"
NÚMEROS: 4 dúzias (48+) de depoimentos positivos
VISUAL: Estratégia de resposta: volume de positivos vs um negativo
[50:52] If you have questions, feel free to ask them as we go into the chat. Um I want to stay in my flow here because I got a lot of shit I got to cover with you guys.
[51:15] When you talked about in the beginning of the uh the video about the bell curve and the four different types of buyers, what are some things that you can do to scale to like the more skeptical um audiences?
[51:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Question - Scaling to Skeptical Audiences"
NÚMEROS: 4 tipos de compradores
VISUAL: Curva de sino, 4 grupos de compradores, foco em céticos
[52:05] Very important to note. Okay. And this is critical to understand. If you don't see the effect, did you say what they actually are thinking that's holding them back?
[52:28] I mean, my assumption is yes, but I haven't seen you see any change in the stats. That's called gaslighting yourself. So, answer the question again.
[52:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Self-Gaslighting in Marketing"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Assumir sem validar, ausência de mudança em métricas como feedback
[53:15] I mean, I guess yeah, the answer would be no because the change hasn't happened. There you go. Now, just to be clear, you therefore have to go through this exercise and consider like what is missing, right?
[53:38] What are the actual things that they're thinking? Like, dude, at the end of the day, let's use John as an example like I have throughout this whole call just for consistency.
[54:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "John Example - Unaddressed Objections"
NÚMEROS: $30K (oferta de John)
VISUAL: Objeções não endereçadas: injetáveis, efeitos colaterais, fertilidade
[54:28] I don't give a fuck about John's background report, right? Like, I don't give a fuck about John hiring a private investigator and showing me his background document. It's not going to get me to convert.
[54:52] I want to know if an injectable is going to shrivel my nuts and make it so I can't have kids and kill me sooner, right? That's literally what I want to know, dude.
[55:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Real Objection vs Assumed Solution"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Objeção real (efeitos de injetáveis) vs solução assumida (background check)
[55:38] Do you genuinely believe that if John took whatever random actions besides addressing that that I would convert? I mean, it didn't address your your your thought process, so no.
[56:05] That's my point. And like, do you think that John is sitting there directly thinking to himself when he sees a cuz all he sees, right? Think about this. All he sees is there's this sales call that didn't close.
[56:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Sales Team Blind Spots"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Vendedor não vê objeção real, apenas vê call não fechado
[56:52] I want you guys to know something. I'll say this as a flex. I'll make a whole fucking video about it as soon as it happens. I am not that far off from just off the education company. A million a month recurring.
[57:15] Do you understand what it takes to just have a million a month recurring? Okay? How dialed in all the messaging has to be?
[57:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Revenue Milestone Context"
NÚMEROS: $1M/mês recorrente
VISUAL: Nível de precisão necessário para receita recorrente de 7 figuras
[58:05] This is your content matrix. Okay? This is where you currently fail with a high rate. Most likely you haven't mapped out the main thing and then all the reasons that people will give a fuck about the main thing.
[58:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Content Matrix - Common Failure Point"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Matriz não mapeada: coisa principal + ramificações de interesse
[58:52] Takes me two hours a week to film YouTube videos, guys. I sit down from 1 to 3 p.m. on Sunday. I'm not better than you, man.
[59:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Time Commitment Reality"
NÚMEROS: 2 horas/semana, Domingo 1-3 PM
VISUAL: Compromisso mínimo de tempo, replicável por qualquer um
[59:38] I film my fucking YouTube videos on an iPad, on a tripod. Okay, 4K resolution, 60 frames a second, an iPad and a fucking tripod.
[1:00:05] So, you're like less than two grand away for a fucking iPad and some microphones and a tripod. And then from there, I pay a guy 3K a month to fucking edit the videos.
[1:00:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Total Content Production Investment"
NÚMEROS: Menos de $2K (equipamento), $3K/mês (editor)
VISUAL: Investimento total acessível, equipamento + serviço mensal
[1:00:52] We have the uh Google AI overview. You guys know what I'm talking about where you Google something and then it gives you that little overview there at the top of the screen.
[1:01:15] That overview is influenced by press and what can be pulled from the search engine. Okay. So, just to be clear, like the ultimate hack to influencing that Google AI overview is what ranks in the search engine.
[1:01:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Google AI Overview Optimization"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Influenciando overview: press releases + conteúdo indexado
[1:02:05] We had a client the other day, you looked up their name and the word scam. And the Google AI overview thing said three fourths of it were like really great things. It was like, "No, this company's not a scam."
[1:02:28] But then the last 1/4 of it, there was, listen to this crazy shit there was a podcast on Apple, like Apple's website that had two one star ratings on the podcast.
[1:02:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Case Study - Negative AI Overview Impact"
NÚMEROS: 3/4 positivo, 1/4 negativo, 2 avaliações 1-estrela, 25% queda show rate
VISUAL: Impacto de conteúdo negativo em AI overview, queda dramática em conversão
[1:03:15] Dude, literally the moment that that ranked in the Google AI search overview, their show rate dropped like 25%. We then obviously took the podcast off of the Apple website completely.
[1:03:38] Which immediately deranked that website, which immediately scrubbed the AI overview information that it pulled from, and that it instant fuckingly raised the show rate back to where it was, right?
[1:04:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Solution - Content Removal Impact"
NÚMEROS: Show rate restaurado após remoção
VISUAL: Ação corretiva imediata, restauração de métricas
[1:04:28] Some of y'all don't even know you have a Reddit thread. Some of y'all still haven't proactively created a Reddit thread and had your fucking customers be the ones that talk about it.
[1:04:52] Some of y'all haven't done exactly what we're sitting here talking about right now and tried to think from your customer's perspective how you look right.
[1:05:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Proactive Reputation Management"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Reddit threads proativos, clientes falando organicamente
[1:05:38] One of my favorite books that he has is this book called Reframing. Okay? And Reframing is an awesome book. If you get the opportunity to buy it, for sure do.
[1:06:05] And the whole concept of reframing is and really all of his books, he consistently demonstrates that he can take people to completely different like views of a situation.
[1:06:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Book Recommendation - Reframing by Richard Bandler"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: NLP, mudança de perspectiva, reframing de crenças
[1:06:52] You have to strategically do this exercise. You have to be able to audit your own brand and business from your consumer's perspective.
[1:07:15] Now, here's the thing. When you hear that, I want to be really clear, guys. How many of you just have one consumer? How many of you literally just have one type of person that you sell to?
[1:07:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Multiple Consumer Perspectives"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Múltiplos tipos de consumidores, não apenas um perfil
[1:08:05] You have multiple consumer perspectives that you can tap into at any moment to view any part of your entire fucking sequence of caring through. And it unlocks as many ideas as you want to take action on.
[1:08:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Unlocking Content Ideas"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Múltiplas perspectivas = ideias ilimitadas de conteúdo
[1:08:52] There's such a substantial difference between those of you sitting here that are like 100 to 300k a month comparatively to the guys who are sitting here that are already at like 2 million plus a month.
[1:09:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Revenue Tiers - Different Needs"
NÚMEROS: $100K-$300K/mês vs $2M+/mês
VISUAL: Diferenças substanciais entre níveis de receita, necessidades distintas
[1:09:38] You're not the same. You have commonalities that you share, but you're also like so different from one another in many ways. And if I want to come up with content ideas or if I want to come up with back-end selling systems.
[1:10:05] All I got to do on my end is pick a customer type and think of the specific part of my process that I want to strengthen.
[1:10:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Content Creation Framework"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Escolher tipo de cliente + parte do processo = ideia de conteúdo
[1:10:52] And so what you do is you do a bottleneck analysis. Why did Omar and I on our call yesterday go onto the search engine and look up Omar HFZ scam.
[1:11:15] Well, cuz he said something about his self-cancellation rate was high and his show rate was low. That's a bottleneck at the exact same point of the process.
[1:11:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Case Study - Omar's Bottleneck"
NÚMEROS: 16% auto-cancelamento
VISUAL: Gargalo identificado: show rate baixo + auto-cancelamento alto
[1:12:05] Omar, what's your self-cancellation rate right now? 16%. Omar's getting people to self-canc at a high rate. Omar's getting people that occasionally don't show up to calls.
[1:12:28] Put a one in the chat. Do you think this influences that show rate and self-cancellation rate? Yes, if you one if you agree, two if you don't agree. Obviously, infinite ones.
[1:12:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Omar's Search Results Problem"
NÚMEROS: 16% self-cancellation rate
VISUAL: Pessoa com mesmo nome processada por DOJ, páginas de resultados negativos
[1:13:15] Omar, do you have any video talking about this? No, I didn't until yesterday. I'm filming it actually like right after this. Omar didn't have a video on this.
[1:13:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Solution - Proactive Content Asset"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Vídeo abordando confusão de nomes, asset proativo para objeção
[1:14:05] There's a sequence of when people care about what. Okay? So, we call this the sequence of caring. When somebody sees an ad, do you think they care to look up Omar Hez scam yet? Yes or no? No.
[1:14:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Sequence of Caring"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Sequência temporal de quando consumidor se importa com o quê
[1:14:52] Between an ad and a VSSL, dude, there's no risk. There's no risk. You just fucking click a button and go to the next page. You're in scanner mode on the front end.
[1:15:15] All you're trying to do on the front end is determining, is this for me or not? Do I do I like this guy's claimed outcomes? Do I resonate with this guy's messaging? That's all the consideration you really have on the front end.
[1:15:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Front-End Consideration"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Modo scanner, baixo risco, apenas determinando relevância
[1:16:05] Between the schedule and between the first call, it goes from consideration to research and it goes to more research.
[1:16:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Research Phase"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Progressão: consideração → pesquisa → mais pesquisa
[1:16:52] This determines where we place that asset. Okay. So, I want to help you guys. I remember I don't want you to be relying on me. I want you to genuinely be able to think on your own.
[1:17:15] In this case, for Omar specifically, it doesn't make sense to put that on the front end. That doesn't make sense for front-end content, right?
[1:17:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Asset Placement Strategy"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Front-end vs back-end placement, timing estratégico de assets
[1:18:05] The better thing to do in that example, like I said, I can't stress this enough. This is the point I'm attempting to make in all of what I'm saying right now. Omar needs a sales asset.
[1:18:28] For when somebody searches that and he needs to determine where is best to proactively give that to people or reactively give it to people when it comes up.
[1:18:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Proactive vs Reactive Asset Delivery"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Duas estratégias: dar antes (proativo) ou depois (reativo) de objeção surgir
[1:19:15] If an objection comes up in the sales process, people hear nothing that you say until their objection gets handled. If a question comes up, nobody hears anything beyond the question until the question get answered.
[1:19:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Objection Handling Priority"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Objeção não resolvida = tudo após é ignorado
[1:20:05] As soon as the whole injectable thing happened on that sales call, I got to be honest, guys, like I like blacked out. I couldn't tell you what the fuck happened after that.
[1:20:28] I didn't pay attention to it because I didn't feel satiated in what I was about to potentially commit to.
[1:20:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Mental Blackout After Unresolved Objection"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Objeção não resolvida causa desconexão mental completa
[1:21:15] You know how I ended the call instead? I was like, "Yeah, his name was Rich." I go, "Yeah, Rich, just shoot me the agreement, man. I'll take a look at it."
[1:21:38] Do you think that guy interpreted me as unqualified? No, dude. Qualified as fuck. Do you think that guy interpreted me as not probable to buy? No, I literally told him I was going to buy. But in reality, like, dude, I'm unsold.
[1:22:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "False Qualification Signal"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Aparenta qualificado, mas não está vendido, objeção silenciosa
[1:22:28] I'll give you the example of like one of these uh one of these raising capital deals that we do. So when these guys, they'll pitch like a real estate offer. I'll use I'll use one of our clients called Lux Locker.
[1:22:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Case Study - Lux Locker"
NÚMEROS: $50K (mínimo), $900K (máximo)
VISUAL: Levantamento de capital para instalações de armazenamento RV/barco
[1:23:15] The average investment at the lowest is 50K. The highest investment is $900,000. If I'm going to give anybody that amount of money, whether it's on the lowest end, the highest end, or somewhere in between, there's a lot of shit I'm going to want to know.
[1:23:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Investment Range"
NÚMEROS: $50K-$900K
VISUAL: Range de investimento, múltiplas perguntas necessárias
[1:24:05] As an example of a sales asset, we run a background check that a private investigator was paid to do. It was thorough. It wasn't one of those $35, $50 fucking background checks.
[1:24:28] This was a private investigator that thoroughly audited these men's lives and determined that they were trustworthy individuals and made a short video on it.
[1:24:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Trust Asset - Private Investigation"
NÚMEROS: Não $35-$50 (checks baratos)
VISUAL: Investigação privada profunda, vídeo como asset de confiança
[1:25:15] Have you ever heard that term before? So, audited financials, I got to be honest in saying like I'm not sure who exactly you pay to like professionally audit your financials.
[1:25:38] That means somebody validated professionally that you're not doing fraudulent activity, okay? and your books are clean and that everything looks right.
[1:26:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Audited Financials"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Validação profissional de livros contábeis, certificação de não-fraude
[1:26:28] Lux Locker paid both those companies to do that. They send those out as trust assets and sales assets. How much more you think that makes people trust the opportunity to invest with these guys?
[1:26:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Lux Locker Trust Strategy"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Audited financials + forensic accountant como assets de vendas
[1:27:15] You see Instagram all the time. They know. They know there's a bunch of fucking kids and teenagers literally running train on them financially, okay? They already know that. That doesn't do anything for them.
[1:27:38] What does stuff for them is like circumstantially placing it where they currently are and making it sound possible, right?
[1:28:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Youth Market - Context Matters"
NÚMEROS: 18-20 anos, objetivo $10K/mês
VISUAL: Contexto circunstancial importa mais que testemunhos gerais
[1:28:28] Like how you going to justify to your family doing this instead of going to school when they think that you should be going to school instead, right?
[1:28:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Specific Objection - Family Approval"
NÚMEROS: 50%+ moram com pais
VISUAL: Objeção específica: aprovação familiar, trabalhar de casa
[1:29:15] Get them to book in. And obviously there's a level of interest to do it. Like right when that occurs, let's start the framing process and like really let these people know we understand exactly where they're at.
[1:29:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Framing Process Post-Booking"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Processo de framing imediato após agendamento
[1:30:05] The only disconnect right now between me and giving John $30,000 is he said everything I'm thinking out loud besides the things I'm thinking about injectables. That's it.
[1:30:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Single Point of Failure"
NÚMEROS: $30K (valor da oferta)
VISUAL: Uma objeção não endereçada = única barreira para conversão
[1:30:52] It's probably the fact that you've disconnected from saying the things that they need to feel you actually relate to and can help them transition through that circumstance to the next layer.
[1:31:15] You're likely just talking about the outcome, okay? Which they resonate with, but what gets them to convert on that outcome is talking about like the part that leads up to the outcome in depth.
[1:31:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Transformation Messaging"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Outcome apenas não converte, precisa abordar jornada até outcome
[1:32:05] Jeremy, completely like off a little bit off topic question. Whenever you were going through that talk, I was like, "Dude, we we haven't posted a YouTube video in like eight months, right?
[1:32:28] Every time I'm like I get in that cycle of wanting to make a video, you know, it looks like shit. It's just not like what I'm wanting it to be.
[1:32:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Perfectionism Paralysis"
NÚMEROS: 8 meses sem postar
VISUAL: Padrões altos impedindo ação, perfeccionismo como bloqueio
[1:33:15] Yeah. 100%. Hell yeah. Then you just have somebody trim it, edit it, ready to go from there. And I don't have to tell them anything, bro. I pay them 3K a month.
[1:33:38] No, just write something down. I just Yeah, I'm just a D1 yapper, you know? You just sit you just again like I'm just talking to the person.
[1:34:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "No Script Needed"
NÚMEROS: $3K/mês (editor)
VISUAL: Sem teleprompter, apenas falar naturalmente com pessoa
[1:34:28] I don't allow my my standards to like hold me back from taking the actions. It's it's better to have it done and in in place comparatively to projecting onto it that it needs to be whatever you're trying to define it needs to be.
[1:34:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Action Over Perfection"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Feito > Perfeito, padrões não devem impedir ação
[1:35:15] I told Omar yesterday, I was like, "Hey, man, for the people who self-cancel, like, why don't you just call him and ask why they canceled?" He thought that was like a revolutionary come to Jesus fucking thing.
[1:35:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Simple Solution - Ask Why"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Solução óbvia: ligar e perguntar por que cancelaram
[1:36:05] Why don't y'all think of this? Because you're not thinking of this basic framework that I'm trying to teach you right now. I'm trying to teach you how to think so you don't have to ask.
[1:36:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Teaching Framework vs Tactics"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Ensinar a pensar vs ensinar o que fazer
[1:36:52] All you're doing is thinking with this framework. What was left unsaid? What could I have sent the person that I didn't? What could I have talked about in more depth that I didn't?
[1:37:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Framework Questions"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Perguntas-chave: O que não foi dito? O que não foi enviado? O que faltou aprofundar?
[1:37:38] Great call. Congratulations. You made it through. Enjoy the day. Get richer. Catch you guys at the Q4 mastermind. Talk soon.
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
$30,000 - Oferta de John (programa com injetáveis)
$10,000-$12,000 - Opção com planos de pagamento
$1M/mês recorrente - Meta próxima de Jeremy (apenas educação)
2 horas/semana - Tempo de filmagem de conteúdo
Domingo 1-3 PM - Horário de gravação
$3K/mês - Custo de editor de vídeo
$5K/mês - Investimento em blogs SEO
Menos de $2K - Equipamento total (iPad, tripé, microfone)
$200 - Microfones Road
4K, 60fps - Qualidade de vídeo
31 anos - Idade de Jeremy
19 anos - Idade que começou no marketing
180 lbs, 6'5" - Peso/altura quando vegano
2019 - Ano de lançamento Inner Circle
16% - Taxa de auto-cancelamento de Omar
25% - Queda de show rate por conteúdo negativo em AI overview
50%+ - Porcentagem de jovens 18-20 que moram com pais
$50K-$900K - Range de investimento Lux Locker
4 dúzias (48+) - Testemunhos online positivos
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Funil Básico: Headline → Video (VSSL) → Application
Sequência de Caring: Ad → VSSL → Application → Schedule → Call 1 → Call 2 → Close
Espectro de Risco: 4 grupos de compradores (1=alto risco, 4=cético extremo)
Camadas de Confiança: Layer 1 (Mensagem) + Layer 2 (Consistência) + Layer 3 (Validação)
Matriz de Conteúdo: Coisa Principal → Ramificações de Razões → Conteúdo Expandido
Repurposing: YouTube (2h) → Editor ($3K) → Instagram/Twitter/LinkedIn automaticamente
SEO/AI Strategy: Blogs ($5K) + Press + Social = Google AI Overview + LLM ranking
Trust Assets: Background checks profissionais + Audited financials + Forensic accountant
Sales Assets: Vídeos específicos abordando objeções proativamente
Placement Strategy: Proativo (antes) vs Reativo (depois) baseado em bottleneck
ESTRUTURA:
Content Matrix Framework:
Centro: Uma Coisa Principal
Ramificações: Todas as razões pelas quais pessoas se importam
Expansão: 15-20 min de vídeo por ramificação
Distribuição: Todos os canais (YouTube, Instagram, Twitter, LinkedIn, Website, Blogs)
Sequence of Caring:
Ad/Descoberta: Modo scanner, sem risco, apenas relevância
VSSL/Página: Consideração inicial
Application: Leve consideração
Schedule: Início de pesquisa
Entre Calls: Pesquisa intensa (Google, Reddit, reviews)
Close: Justificação financeira
Bottleneck Analysis Process:
Identificar métrica problemática
Escolher tipo específico de consumidor
Pensar do ponto de vista deles
Identificar o que está faltando
Criar asset específico
Determinar placement (proativo vs reativo)
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Investimento Mensal Total em Conteúdo: ~$8K
$3K - Editor de vídeo
$5K - Blogs SEO
Investimento Único em Equipamento: <$2K
ROI Objetivo: $1M/mês recorrente (educação)
Impacto de Reputation Management: 25% variação em show rate
Faixa de Receita Inner Circle: $100K-$300K/mês (baixo) vs $2M+/mês (alto)
DURAÇÃO: 1:37:38 FORMATO: Webinar/Masterclass ao vivo com exercícios práticos
